Public search was used as a fallback cross-check to confirm that the source set is on official Heidelberg University domains.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery located official Heidelberg University evidence for AI use in study and teaching, doctoral dissertation guidance, and university-hosted AI services. Approved claim evidence was limited to the German AI guideline PDF, the official heiSKILLS teaching page, the Graduate Academy dissertation page, and the URZ AI/YoKI page. Tool profiles, self-learning catalogs, Moodle courses, news posts, and library catalog records were rejected as low-specificity, course-only, login/inaccessible, or non-policy sources.

artifacts.claimText: For study and teaching at Heidelberg University, AI and language models such as ChatGPT or DeepL are generally allowed only within rules set by examiners or instructors, and the independent character of the student's work must be preserved.

citation.citationTitle: Leitlinie zum Umgang mit KI in Studium und Lehre an der Universität Heidelberg
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For study work, Heidelberg University requires students to submit an independence and AI-use declaration that discloses AI tool use and includes an overview of the tools used and their purposes.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Heidelberg University's AI guideline advises instructors and students not to enter sensitive, personal, confidential, or copyrighted text into AI systems that could store or process it outside Heidelberg University infrastructure.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Leitlinie zum Umgang mit KI in Studium und Lehre an der Universität Heidelberg
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Die Nutzung von KI und Sprachmodellen (LLMs) wie ChatGPT, DeepL oder anderen Werkzeugen ist im Studium und in der Lehre an der UHD grundsätzlich erlaubt. Die spezifische Anwendung muss mit den Prüfer:innen und Lehrenden abgestimmt werden. Zentral ist, dass ein eigenständiger Charakter der Leistung gewahrt bleibt.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Use of AI and language models is generally allowed in study and teaching, but specific use must be coordinated with examiners/instructors and independent work must remain intact.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: PDF page 3, section III.1 Zulässigkeit der KI-Nutzung
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Leitlinie zum Umgang mit KI in Studium und Lehre an der Universität Heidelberg
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Leitlinie zum Umgang mit KI in Studium und Lehre an der Universität Heidelberg
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Studierende müssen für alle Studienarbeiten eine Eigenständigkeits- und Kennzeichnungserklärung abgeben, die den Einsatz von KI-Werkzeugen offenlegt. Diese Kennzeichnungspflicht gilt für Text/Textteile, Bilder, Videos und Musikstücke sowie alle anderen Elemente, für deren Erstellung KI verwendet wurde. Eine Übersicht der genutzten KI-Tools und deren Verwendungszweck ist verpflichtend.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students must submit an independence and AI-use declaration for study work, and the overview of AI tools used and their purposes is mandatory.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Leitlinie zum Umgang mit KI in Studium und Lehre an der Universität Heidelberg
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Lehrende und Studierende sollten bei der Nutzung von KI darauf achten, dass keine sensiblen, personenbezogenen oder vertraulichen Daten sowie urheberrechtlich geschützte Texte/Textteile in KI-Systeme eingegeben werden, die diese Daten außerhalb der Infrastruktur der Universität Heidelberg speichern oder verarbeiten könnten.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Instructors and students should avoid entering sensitive, personal, confidential, or copyrighted text into AI systems that could store or process it outside Heidelberg University infrastructure.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Use of Generative AI in Doctoral Dissertations - Embracing Innovation with Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of AI tools must be explicitly disclosed, specifying when, where, why, and to what extent they were employed in the preparation of the dissertation. AI tools must be used as assistive instruments, not as substitutes for independent intellectual work, critical thinking, or scholarly judgment.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Doctoral AI use must be disclosed with when, where, why, and extent, and AI tools are assistive instruments rather than substitutes for independent scholarly work.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Transparency and Disclosure; Academic Autonomy and Integrity sections

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Um Beschäftigten, Studierenden und Forschenden einen sicheren Zugang zu generativen KI-Modellen zu gewährleisten und diese im Arbeitsalltag konstruktiv zu integrieren, entwickelt und hostet das URZ eigene KI-Intrastruktur und setzt Open-Source-Produkte ein. Über die KI-Plattform YoKI können Universitätsmitglieder aus verschiedenen großen Sprachmodellen auswählen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: URZ says it develops and hosts its own AI infrastructure and offers YoKI so university members can choose from several large language models.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim because the German guideline directly states general permissibility, coordination with examiners/instructors, and preserved independent work.
Approved because the source directly requires an independence and AI-use declaration and a mandatory overview of tools and purposes for study work.
Approved with advisory wording because the German source says instructors and students should avoid entering sensitive, personal, confidential, or copyrighted material into external AI systems.
Approved with 'asked to' wording rather than a stronger mandate because the German source uses 'dazu angehalten'.
Approved because the Graduate Academy page directly states disclosure requirements for doctoral dissertations and says AI tools must remain assistive instruments.
Approved as a service/status claim because the URZ page directly states that URZ develops and hosts its own AI infrastructure and provides YoKI to university members.

artifacts.title: Universität Heidelberg AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Universität Heidelberg is based on official public university sources. The German AI guideline supports claims on conditional AI permissibility in study and teaching, AI-use disclosure, data-entry cautions, and instructor communication of allowed/prohibited AI uses. The Graduate Academy page supports a doctoral-dissertation disclosure and authorial-responsibility claim. The URZ page supports a narrow claim about the university-hosted YoKI AI platform. Rejected sources include tool profiles, self-learning catalogs, news posts, Moodle course links, and library catalog records that were too generic, inaccessible, course-only, or non-policy-specific.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Evidence is limited to public official Heidelberg University pages and PDFs available without login; Moodle/internal-course materials and external resource catalogs were not used as policy evidence.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human-reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
